subject: Economics
chapter: Employment: Growth, Informalisation and Other Issues

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Informal economy is not always associated with low productivity.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Informal economy is associated with low productivity in many cases, but there are instances where skills acquired in informal economy are utilized to increase productivity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Informal economy is not always associated with low productivity.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The informal sector is considered the primary source of employment in many developing countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The formal sector is often limited in developing countries, making the informal sector the primary source of employment.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The informal economy is often associated with low-skilled and low-wage jobs.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many informal economy jobs require specialized skills and pay decent wages.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The concept of employment is often linked to the formal sector.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many jobs in the informal sector also provide employment for individuals.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Poverty is a major factor contributing to unemployment in developing countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Poverty can lead to low purchasing power, reducing the demand for goods and services, which in turn can lead to unemployment.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The informal economy is often associated with low productivity.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many informal economy jobs require specialized skills and pay decent wages.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Employment growth is often linked to economic growth.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Economic growth can lead to an increase in the demand for goods and services, which in turn can lead to an increase in employment.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The informal sector is not regulated by the government.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many governments regulate certain aspects of the informal sector, such as labor laws and taxation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Unemployment is a major problem in developing countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Unemployment is a major problem in both developing and developed countries.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The informal sector is a major source of employment for women.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many women work in the formal sector as well.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
